user_query:
I'm an Iowa voter getting ready to speak at a county meeting about local river water quality. Please analyze my nitrate data and help me finalize materials for outreach and testimony using only the files in this workspace.

Inputs to use:
- input/iowa_river_nitrate_monthly.csv (monthly averages and sample counts)
- input/notes.txt (meeting details, tone, and the nitrate threshold to use)
- input/community_draft_letter.md (a draft I want you to complete and polish)

Required outputs and constraints:
1) Create outputs/last12_records.csv containing only the 12 most recent months from input/iowa_river_nitrate_monthly.csv, preserving the same columns and order as the input.
2) Create outputs/last12_metrics.csv with two columns: metric,value. Include at least these rows computed from the 12-month subset:
   - last12_avg_mg_L (mean of avg_nitrate_mg_L, rounded to 1 decimal)
   - last12_max_mg_L (maximum avg_nitrate_mg_L, rounded to 1 decimal)
   - last12_max_month (format as Month YYYY for the row with the maximum)
   - last12_count_exceeding_limit (number of months at or above the nitrate limit read from notes.txt)
   - last12_total_samples (sum of samples across the 12 months)
   Use the nitrate limit value stated in input/notes.txt; do not hardcode any threshold.
3) Edit the draft letter at input/community_draft_letter.md and save the result to outputs/community_letter_updated.md by:
   - Replacing all placeholders (e.g., {{LAST_12M_AVG}}, {{EPA_LIMIT}}, {{NUM_EXCEED}}, {{MAX_MONTH_LABEL}}, {{MAX_VALUE}}, {{MEETING_WHEN}}, {{MEETING_WHERE}}) with the correct values derived from your analysis and input/notes.txt. For mg/L figures, use the same rounding as in outputs/last12_metrics.csv where applicable.
   - Rewriting the "Ask" section into one clear, specific, and constructive paragraph that covers the three points already listed in the draft. Keep the rest of the letter structure intact and improve clarity as needed, following the tone guidance in notes.txt. Spell out EPA on first mention as "U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)."
4) Compose a short neighborhood status update (120–180 words) summarizing what the data show over the most recent 12 months, how it compares to the limit from notes.txt, the highest month and value, and inviting neighbors to the meeting. Include the meeting date/time and location from notes.txt. Save this to outputs/status_update.txt.

General rules:
- Define the "last 12 months" strictly as the 12 most recent rows by date in input/iowa_river_nitrate_monthly.csv.
- Do not fetch any external data; rely only on the provided files.
- Ensure all outputs are internally consistent (e.g., the max month/value in the letter and status update match outputs/last12_metrics.csv and appear in outputs/last12_records.csv). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
